commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review should choose the canonical DSML recovery branch and require contributor-supplied runtime proof plus the wrapper-kind and capture-boundary fixes before merge.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a concrete security-boundary concern because hidden provider text can become executable tool calls without preserving the DSML wrapper kind.; Review finding: [P1] Keep error DSML wrappers non-executable... (sha=b9f1d2be4b6ba238dcc8370054709a428dee92b9)
commands.review_summary:
The branch extends the DeepSeek text filter to capture DSML markup, parse invokes into synthetic tool calls, inject recovered calls into the OpenAI completions stream when native tool calls are absent, and adds parser/filter tests.

PR surface: Source +110, Tests +94. Total +204 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers: source inspection shows `tool_use_error` is captured and then parsed without wrapper identity, and `dsmlCapture` appends without a cap. The original live DeepSeek failure is supported by related reports, but I did not run a live model in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should choose the canonical DSML recovery branch and require contributor-supplied runtime proof plus the wrapper-kind and capture-boundary fixes before merge.

target.title: fix(agents): recover tool calls from DeepSeek DSML text markup
target.author: sebtardif
